Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_raw: { "opinion": "As a hairdresser in New York, I've noticed how fake content on social media can really throw people off. I mean, I see it every day when clients come in asking for impossible looks they've seen online. But I also see how people might feel misled or anxious because of it. So, to manage this, I think one of the best ways is to be more mindful about what you consume. I tell my clients to take a step back and ask themselves, 'Does this make sense? Can this really be true?' It's important to question the source too. Maybe the person posting has a hidden agenda or is just trying to get attention. Another thing I've found helpful is fact-checking. There are tools and websites that can help verify information. I encourage people to use these before sharing anything. Also, being selective about who you follow can make a big difference. Unfollowing accounts that consistently post unreliable content can create a healthier social media environment. Lastly, I think fostering a community where people share accurate and positive information can really help combat fake content. It's all about taking control of what you engage with and promoting a culture of truth and kindness online.", "reasoning_summary": "The user is a 54-year-old hairdresser with a high level of openness and extraversion. response_parsed.opinion: As a hairdresser in New York, I've noticed how fake content on social media can really throw people off. I mean, I see it every day when clients come in asking for impossible looks they've seen online. But I also see how people might feel misled or anxious because of it. So, to manage this, I think one of the best ways is to be more mindful about what you consume. I tell my clients to take a step back and ask themselves, 'Does this make sense? Can this really be true?' It's important to question the source too. Maybe the person posting has a hidden agenda or is just trying to get attention. Another thing I've found helpful is fact-checking. There are tools and websites that can help verify information. I encourage people to use these before sharing anything. Also, being selective about who you follow can make a big difference. Unfollowing accounts that consistently post unreliable content can create a healthier social media environment. Lastly, I think fostering a community where people share accurate and positive information can really help combat fake content. It's all about taking control of what you engage with and promoting a culture of truth and kindness online.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The user is a 54-year-old hairdresser with a high level of openness and extraversion. She is self-employed in New York and spends a few times weekly on social media. Her hobbies include baking, reading novels, and painting. Given her background, she interacts with a diverse client base and is attuned to societal trends. Her perspective on managing fake content involves personal experience with clients being misled by social media. Her high openness suggests she is open to new ideas and experiences, which influences her proactive approach to addressing fake content. Her extraversion means she is likely to engage with others and spread awareness about this issue. Her moderate conscientiousness and agreeableness indicate she is methodical and approachable, which helps her provide practical solutions. Her low neuroticism implies she is emotionally stable and can handle stress related to fake content without anxiety.
